old_claim.note: Quantum Physics is a plausible dependency for a forecast technology and should be treated as speculative.

new_claim.note: Quantum effects help explain why nanoscale materials can have size-dependent properties; this is a shared scientific foundation rather than a product-style prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: NNI page section explaining that quantum effects can rule behavior and properties at the nanoscale.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Nanoscale behavior can be governed by quantum effects.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports quantum physics as a shared explanatory foundation, not as a speculative future-only prerequisite.

ontology_before: The edge treated nanotechnology as an unsourced 2035 forecast dependency.
ontology_after: The edge connects established nanotechnology to quantum physics as a common scientific foundation.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to purely institutional program coordination with no scientific-scope claim.
A source showed the graph should model only manufactured products rather than scientific fields.
short_reason: Quantum physics is a shared foundation for nanoscale effects.
